claim: Blocking rules take precedence over warnings; warnings dropped if any block matched
Rule is blocking only when action=='block', else warning
All regex matching is case-insensitive (re.IGNORECASE hardcoded, lru_cache 128)
Tool matcher '*' any; else split '|' and exact membership (OR, not regex)
Legacy 'pattern' becomes one regex_match condition only when no conditions; field inferred from event
Rule discovery is CWD-relative glob of .claude/hookify.*.local.md
note: running from another dir loads no rules

claim: stop uses fixed event 'stop', userpromptsubmit uses fixed event 'prompt'
Hooks always sys.exit(0) in finally; errors never block operation
action 'block' documented as (future) but rule_engine already implements blocking
note: latent inconsistency vs core/rule_engine.py:55-61; moved to Unverified/Needs-review
